content.problemStatement:
MoEngage has given you two trees T1T_1T1‚Äč and T2T_2T2‚Äč. Both trees have NNN nodes and are rooted at node 111.

Definitions:


LCA1(u,v)=LCA_1(u,v) = LCA1‚Äč(u,v)= Lowest common ancestor of node uuu and node vvv in tree T1T_1T1‚Äč.
LCA2(u,v)=LCA_2(u,v) = LCA2‚Äč(u,v)= Lowest common ancestor of node uuu and node vvv in tree T2T_2T2‚Äč.
H1(v)=H_1(v) = H1‚Äč(v)= Number of nodes in the shortest path from node 111 to node vvv in tree T1T_1T1‚Äč. Note that, H1(1)=1H_1(1) = 1H1‚Äč(1)=1.
H2(v)=H_2(v) = H2‚Äč(v)= Number of nodes in the shortest path from node 111 to node vvv in tree T2T_2T2‚Äč. Note that, H2(1)=1H_2(1) = 1H2‚Äč(1)=1.
Find the **maximum** value of H1(LCA1(u,v))+H2(LCA2(u,v))H_1(LCA_1(u,v)) + H_2(LCA_2(u,v))H1‚Äč(LCA1‚Äč(u,v))+H2‚Äč(LCA2‚Äč(u,v)) over all pairs (u,v)(u,v)(u,v) such that u‚Č†vu \neq vuÓÄ†=v.

More formally, find max‚Ā°1‚Č§u<v‚Č§N[H1(LCA1(u,v))+H2(LCA2(u,v))]\max\limits_{1 \leq u \lt v \leq N} [H_1(LCA_1(u,v)) + H_2(LCA_2(u,v))]1‚Č§u<v‚Č§Nmax‚Äč[H1‚Äč(LCA1‚Äč(u,v))+H2‚Äč(LCA2‚Äč(u,v))].


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of nodes.
Each of the next (N‚ąí1)(N - 1)(N‚ąí1) lines contain two integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between nodes uuu and vvv in tree T1T_1T1‚Äč.
Each of the next (N‚ąí1)(N - 1)(N‚ąí1) lines contain two integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between nodes uuu and vvv in tree T2T_2T2‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line max‚Ā°1‚Č§u<v‚Č§N[H1(LCA1(u,v))+H2(LCA2(u,v))]\max\limits_{1 \leq u \lt v \leq N} [H_1(LCA_1(u,v)) + H_2(LCA_2(u,v))]1‚Č§u<v‚Č§Nmax‚Äč[H1‚Äč(LCA1‚Äč(u,v))+H2‚Äč(LCA2‚Äč(u,v))].
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The trees T1T_1T1‚Äč and T2T_2T2‚Äč are:


For u=3u = 3u=3 and v=5v=5v=5, LCA1(3,5)=3LCA_1(3,5) = 3LCA1‚Äč(3,5)=3, LCA2(3,5)=3LCA_2(3,5) = 3LCA2‚Äč(3,5)=3, H1(3)=2,H2(3)=3H_1(3) = 2,H_2(3) = 3H1‚Äč(3)=2,H2‚Äč(3)=3. Thus, H1(LCA1(3,5))+H2(LCA2(3,5))=H1(3)+H2(3)=5H_1(LCA_1(3,5)) + H_2(LCA_2(3,5)) = H_1(3) + H_2(3) = 5H1‚Äč(LCA1‚Äč(3,5))+H2‚Äč(LCA2‚Äč(3,5))=H1‚Äč(3)+H2‚Äč(3)=5.
It can be proven that 555 is the maximum possible answer for this case amongst all pairs (u,v)(u,v)(u,v).

Test case 222: The trees T1T_1T1‚Äč and T2T_2T2‚Äč are:


For u=2u = 2u=2 and v=4v=4v=4, LCA1(2,4)=2LCA_1(2,4) = 2LCA1‚Äč(2,4)=2, LCA2(2,4)=1LCA_2(2,4) = 1LCA2‚Äč(2,4)=1, H1(2)=2,H2(1)=1H_1(2) = 2,H_2(1) = 1H1‚Äč(2)=2,H2‚Äč(1)=1. Thus, H1(LCA1(2,4))+H2(LCA2(2,4))=H1(2)+H2(1)=3H_1(LCA_1(2,4)) + H_2(LCA_2(2,4)) = H_1(2) + H_2(1) = 3H1‚Äč(LCA1‚Äč(2,4))+H2‚Äč(LCA2‚Äč(2,4))=H1‚Äč(2)+H2‚Äč(1)=3.
It can be proven that 333 is the maximum possible answer for this case amongst all pairs (u,v)(u,v)(u,v).
